import pytest
from collections import OrderedDict

def lru_cache(capacity: int):
    from collections import OrderedDict
    cache = OrderedDict()
    def get(key):
        if key in cache:
            cache.move_to_end(key)
            return cache[key]
        return -1
    def put(key, value):
        if key in cache:
            cache.move_to_end(key)
        cache[key] = value
        if len(cache) > capacity:
            cache.popitem(last=False)
    return get, put

class TestLRUCache:
    
    def test_happy_path_basic_operations(self):
        """Test basic get and put operations"""
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        
        assert get(1) == 1  # Returns 1
        put(3, 3)           # Evicts key 2
        assert get(2) == -1 # Returns -1 (not found)
        put(4, 4)           # Evicts key 1
        assert get(1) == -1 # Returns -1 (not found)
        assert get(3) == 3  # Returns 3
        assert get(4) == 4  # Returns 4
    
    def test_happy_path_lru_eviction(self):
        """Test that least recently used item is evicted"""
        get, put = lru_cache(3)
        
        put(1, "a")
        put(2, "b")
        put(3, "c")
        
        # Access key 1 to make it most recently used
        assert get(1) == "a"
        
        # Add new item - should evict key 2 (least recently used)
        put(4, "d")
        
        assert get(1) == "a"  # Still exists (was recently used)
        assert get(2) == -1   # Evicted
        assert get(3) == "c"  # Still exists
        assert get(4) == "d"  # New item exists
    
    def test_update_existing_key(self):
        """Test updating value of existing key"""
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
        
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        assert get(1) == 1
        
        # Update existing key
        put(1, 10)
        assert get(1) == 10
        assert get(2) == 2
        
        # Add new key - should evict key 2 (not key 1, even though key 1 was updated)
        put(3, 3)
        assert get(1) == 10  # Still exists
        assert get(2) == -1  # Evicted
        assert get(3) == 3   # New item
    
    def test_capacity_1(self):
        """Test with capacity of 1"""
        get, put = lru_cache(1)
        
        put(1, 1)
        assert get(1) == 1
        
        put(2, 2)  # Should evict key 1
        assert get(1) == -1
        assert get(2) == 2
        
        put(2, 20)  # Update same key
        assert get(2) == 20
    
    def test_capacity_0(self):
        """Test with capacity of 0"""
        get, put = lru_cache(0)
        
        put(1, 1)
        assert get(1) == -1  # Should immediately be evicted
    
    def test_same_key_accessed_multiple_times(self):
        """Test accessing the same key multiple times"""
        get, put = lru_cache(3)
        
        put(1, 1)
        put(2, 2)
        put(3, 3)
        
        # Access key 1 multiple times
        assert get(1) == 1
        assert get(1) == 1
        assert get(1) == 1
        
        # Add new item - should evict key 2 (key 1 was accessed recently)
        put(4, 4)
        assert get(1) == 1   # Still exists
        assert get(2) == -1  # Evicted
        assert get(3) == 3   # Still exists
        assert get(4) == 4   # New item
    
    def test_get_nonexistent_key(self):
        """Test getting a key that doesn't exist"""
        get, put = lru_cache(2)
